# Â§7. Automated check â€” `scitex-dev ecosystem audit-mcp-tools`

> **Status â€” shipped (2026-04-30).** Static + behavioral checks for Â§1â€“Â§6 implemented in `scitex_dev._cli_audit._mcp_audit`. Tests in `tests/test_cli_audit_mcp.py`. The Â§9 [10_audit-checklist.md](10_audit-checklist.md) remains the manual fallback for anything not yet automated.

- Opt-in linter that walks every `scitex-*` package's `_mcp_server.mcp` instance.
- Warns (never errors) on Â§1â€“Â§6 violations.
- Behind the `cli-audit` extra (shared with `audit-cli`).

```bash
pip install 'scitex-dev[cli-audit]'
scitex-dev ecosystem audit-mcp-tools <package-name>
scitex-dev ecosystem audit-mcp-tools --all --json > mcp-drift.json
scitex-dev ecosystem audit-mcp-tools <pkg> --behavioral   # also probe `<cli> mcp ...`
```

## Coverage matrix

| Rule                                                                  | Section | Status      | Notes                                                                |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|---------|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Single `FastMCP` instance at `scitex_<pkg>._mcp_server.mcp`           | Â§1      | âś… static   | `_resolve_mcp_server` tries `_mcp_server`, `mcp_server`, `_mcp.server`, `mcp.server`. |
| Umbrella bridge uses `safe_mount` (not hand-wrap, not direct `.mount`) | Â§1     | âś… static   | Two distinct Â§1 violations: hand-wrap (per-tool `@mcp.tool()`) and direct `mcp.mount(...)` without `safe_mount`. |
| No double prefix (`<pkg>_<pkg>_*`)                                    | Â§1      | âś… static   | Per-tool name check.                                                  |
| Standalone tools use bare names when umbrella mounts with namespace   | Â§1/Â§2   | TODO        | If umbrella bridge calls `safe_mount(..., namespace="<pkg>")`, every tool registered on `scitex_<pkg>._mcp_server.mcp` must NOT start with `<pkg>_`. Auditor today only catches the umbrella half (hand-wrap); add the inverse to flag pre-prefixed standalone tools (the scitex-dataset 2026-05-06 case â€” 21 `dataset_<x>` tools renamed at once). |
| Tool naming `<pkg>_<verb>_<noun>` snake_case                          | Â§2      | âś… static   | snake_case + verb/noun split + bare-verb-allowlist for `_save`/`_speak`/etc. |
| Required subcommands (`mcp start | doctor | list-tools | install`) on the CLI | Â§3 | âś… static + behavioral | **Static:** click-tree walk verifies all four leaves under the `mcp` group; missing any one is a Â§3 violation (the scitex-scholar pre-2026-05-06 case shipped only `list-tools` and went unflagged because the check was behavioral-only). **Behavioral:** under `--behavioral`, also probes `<cli> mcp <sub> --help` to confirm the leaf actually wires up. |
| `mcp list-tools` accepts `-v|-vv|-vvv` and `--json`                   | Â§4      | âś… behavioral | Monotonic ladder check + JSON parseability.                          |
| `<pkg>_skills_list` and `<pkg>_skills_get` present                    | Â§5      | âś… static   | Accepts both prefixed and bare (Convention A vs B).                  |
| Every public Python API has matching MCP tool                         | Â§6      | âś… static   | Functions only (classes filtered); >50% missing â†’ flag.              |
| Every MCP tool traces back to a public Python API (no orphans)        | Â§6      | âś… static   | Skill-tool envelope (`skills_*`) excluded; >3 orphans â†’ flag.        |
| No banned synonyms in tool names (`io_ls`, `io_rm`, â€¦)                | Â§2      | âś… static   | Maps `ls`â†’`list`, `rm`â†’`delete`, `display`â†’`show`, etc.              |
| JSON-schema arg parity at `-vvv`                                      | Â§6      | âťŚ TODO     | Currently only verifies `--json` parses, not arg-name/type match.    |

The matrix layout mirrors `audit-cli` ([CLI Â§1d](../02_cli/07_audit-cli.md)).

## What it should flag

- **Mount drift.** Bridge file in `scitex/_mcp_tools/<pkg>.py` hand-wraps tools (any `@mcp.tool()` decorator) instead of `safe_mount`.
- **Standalone-prefix drift.** Standalone `_mcp_server.mcp` registers a tool whose name starts with `<pkg>_` while the umbrella bridge mounts with `namespace="<pkg>"` â€” strip the prefix in the standalone (the namespace is added at mount time).
- **Double prefix.** Tool names `<pkg>_<pkg>_*` after mount.
- **Banned bare leaves.** Tool name = `<pkg>` only (no verb), or `<pkg>_<verb>` only with no noun where transitive.
- **Synonyms.** `<pkg>_ls` instead of `<pkg>_list`; `<pkg>_rm` instead of `<pkg>_delete`. Same "Avoid" column as the CLI catalog.
- **Parity gap.** Public Python API exists but no MCP tool wraps it (or vice versa: orphan MCP tool with no matching API).
- **Missing skills tools.** `<pkg>_skills_list` / `<pkg>_skills_get` absent.
- **Missing `mcp` subcommands** on the CLI (`start`, `doctor`, `list-tools`, `show-installation`).
- **`mcp list-tools` ladder broken.** `-vv` output âŠ„ `-v` output (level not additive).

## Output and exit code

- **Output:** human-readable findings on stderr; machine-readable summary on stdout when `--json` passed.
- **Exit code:** always `0` â€” warns, doesn't fail. `--strict` (future) flips warnings to errors for CI gating.

## Operational notes

- Run in CI for every `scitex-*` repo.
- Parity check requires both the package AND the umbrella to be installed (so `list-python-apis` and `mcp list-tools` can both run).
- Skip pass-through entries (same exemption mechanism as the CLI auditor â€” see [CLI Â§1c](../02_cli/05_pass-through.md)).
